title: How Startups and SMBs Are Competing with Enterprise-Level AI
author: Boon Lai, Mahvish Khan

sections.heading: Key Takeaways
sections.text: AI is accessible to small businesses, with 53% of SMBs and startups already using AI, closing the gap with larger enterprise.It improves productivity and customer experience by automating tasks and helps create seamless customer journeys.AI is a job-creator: 59% of SMBs have created new jobs due to AI adoption, with 58% expecting to create more. Artificial intelligence (AI)isn’t just for big corporations anymore. It’s now affordable and easy to use. Small teams can create amazing customer experiences while competing with industry giants and staying special with speed and personal touch. According toSalesforce AI Research, 53% of small businesses already use AI. The gap between big and small businesses is closing fast. The question isn’t whether you should use AI — it’s how quickly you can start. Businesses that act now will gain a competitive edge, and those who wait will get left behind. Here’s how to level up your business: What we’ll cover: Maximize productivity wins with AI automationInvest strategically in AI that scalesDeliver enterprise-grade customer experiences with AIPrepare for AI-powered jobsHow are SMBs outpacing enterprise? You guessed it: AI Your competition may have bigger budgets and larger teams. But with AI, you get the power of big tools and more resources. Modern AI tools put enterprise-level power directly into the hands ofsmall and medium-sized businesses (SMBs). Let’s find out how.

inline_links.text: Maximize productivity wins with AI automation
Invest strategically in AI that scales
Deliver enterprise-grade customer experiences with AI
How are SMBs outpacing enterprise? You guessed it: AI

sections.heading: Maximize productivity wins with AI automation
sections.text: Our research shows that,85% of SMBs believe AI can improve productivity and collaboration. When you’re running a lean team but want enterprise-level results, every minute counts. AI turns time-consuming tasks into automated workflows that run while you focus on growing your business. How enterprises handle productivity:Enterprises solve productivity problems by adding more people and deploying multiple complex tools across departments. They have dedicated teams for data entry, separate departments for report generation, and specialized divisions using advanced software systems. When something breaks, they assign committees and implement new technical solutions. When they need insights, they hire analysts and deploy analytics platforms. Their productivity comes from having enough people and sophisticated tools to handle every task. How SMBs handle productivity: Small teams can now operate with enterprise-scale efficiency. Smart business tools likecustomer relationship management (CRM)are now handling repetitive tasks, organizing information, and connecting team members instantly. With the right tools, your 10-person team can deliver the same output quality as a 500-person enterprise department. CRMs automate sales, marketing, and service efforts like lead scoring and drip email campaigns, handling routine inquiries, so teams stay focused on strategy and growth.Slack, one of the top productivity tools for most businesses, is free and summarizes conversations, finds key decisions, and speeds up collaboration across departments. These capabilities used to require entire marketing teams with data analysts and campaign specialists. Now they’re built into platforms you already use, giving you enterprise capabilities without the complexity.

inline_links.text: 85% of SMBs believe AI can improve productivity and collaboration

sections.heading: Strategically invest in AI that scales
sections.text: 64% of small businesses pay more for AI-powered applications because they see results. Smart leaders aren’t buying every new tool, they’re choosing the AI that‌ improves how they work overall. How enterprises invest in AI:Big organizations can afford to experiment with dozens of AI tools. They can hire dedicated data science teams, and build custom solutions from scratch. They have separate budgets for pilot programs, proof-of-concepts, and full implementations. Their AI investments often take years to show results, but they can absorb those timelines and costs. How SMBs invest in AI:Only 40% of small businesses currently use AI, but they start with solutions costing less than streaming services. They begin with simple tools like email workflows or chatbots, then gradually add capabilities as platforms evolve. Each successful step funds the next upgrade‌ — ‌sales teams automate lead qualification, marketing schedules, campaigns, service deployments, and response tools. The platform guides this progression, making advanced AI accessible without enterprise complexity. Sales:AI sales toolshelp show your team which prospects are ready to buy and when to contact them and learn from your successful deals to predict future wins.Marketing:AI marketing toolscreate personalized experiences andA/B testcampaigns before launch for better results.Service:AI service toolsget instant answers to common questions and automatic routing for complex issues. The smart move is to invest in a smart CRM now, and scale up to AI from there. A solid CRM foundation gives you clean data, and integrated workflows that make AI exponentially more powerful. By starting with aCRM for SMBs, you’ll prepare your data for advanced tools and technologies in the future, helping you fuel business growth faster.Starter Suiteincludes Data 360 features built-in, so you get both the CRM foundation andautomationcapabilities in one strategic investment that grows with your business.

How enterprises deliver customer experiences:Enterprises have AI-powered insights, dedicated customer experience teams, separate departments for sales enablement, marketing operations, and customer success. They can afford 24/7 call centers, personalized account managers, and sophisticatedcustomer journeymapping. Their customer experience comes from having specialized people for every touchpoint and the right data to back it up. These teams consolidate sales, marketing, and service operations into integrated systems where every customer touchpoint builds on previous interactions. Automatedlead scoringenables focused prospecting, whilemarketingautomation delivers personalized engagement at scale. But it’s theserviceexperience that really shines with the right CRM (shall we say 24/7 chatbot?). Service automation resolves common issues instantly and routes complex cases to specialists. It creates the 24/7 support experience customers expect from big enterprises.AI chatbotsanswer questions immediately. Smart routing gets problems to the right person fast. Your customers get enterprise-level support from your small team. Moderncustomer service CRMplatforms include these customer-facing smart tools, so you can deliver enterprise-quality experiences without hiring specialists or paying consulting fees.

raw_html:
Key Takeaways

  • AI is accessible to small businesses, with 53% of SMBs and startups already using AI, closing the gap with larger enterprise.
  • It improves productivity and customer experience by automating tasks and helps create seamless customer journeys.
  • AI is a job-creator: 59% of SMBs have created new jobs due to AI adoption, with 58% expecting to create more.
This summary was created with AI and reviewed by an editor.

Artificial intelligence (AI) isn’t just for big corporations anymore. It’s now affordable and easy to use. Small teams can create amazing customer experiences while competing with industry giants and staying special with speed and personal touch.

According to Salesforce AI Research, 53% of small businesses already use AI. The gap between big and small businesses is closing fast. The question isn’t whether you should use AI — it’s how quickly you can start. Businesses that act now will gain a competitive edge, and those who wait will get left behind. Here’s how to level up your business:

What we’ll cover:

  • Maximize productivity wins with AI automation
  • Invest strategically in AI that scales
  • Deliver enterprise-grade customer experiences with AI
  • Prepare for AI-powered jobs
  • How are SMBs outpacing enterprise? You guessed it: AI

Your competition may have bigger budgets and larger teams. But with AI, you get the power of big tools and more resources. Modern AI tools put enterprise-level power directly into the hands of small and medium-sized businesses (SMBs). Let’s find out how. 

Maximize productivity wins with AI automation

Our research shows that, 85% of SMBs believe AI can improve productivity and collaboration. When you’re running a lean team but want enterprise-level results, every minute counts. AI turns time-consuming tasks into automated workflows that run while you focus on growing your business.

How enterprises handle productivity: Enterprises solve productivity problems by adding more people and deploying multiple complex tools across departments. They have dedicated teams for data entry, separate departments for report generation, and specialized divisions using advanced software systems. When something breaks, they assign committees and implement new technical solutions. When they need insights, they hire analysts and deploy analytics platforms. Their productivity comes from having enough people and sophisticated tools to handle every task.

How SMBs handle productivity: Small teams can now operate with enterprise-scale efficiency. Smart business tools like customer relationship management (CRM) are now handling repetitive tasks, organizing information, and connecting team members instantly. With the right tools, your 10-person team can deliver the same output quality as a 500-person enterprise department.

  • CRMs automate sales, marketing, and service efforts like lead scoring and drip email campaigns, handling routine inquiries, so teams stay focused on strategy and growth.
  • Slack, one of the top productivity tools for most businesses, is free and summarizes conversations, finds key decisions, and speeds up collaboration across departments.

These capabilities used to require entire marketing teams with data analysts and campaign specialists. Now they’re built into platforms you already use, giving you enterprise capabilities without the complexity.

AI Tools for Small Business

Get Your Data Ready For AI

What is Agentforce 360 For Small Business?

Grow Your Startup or SMB With AI Agents

Kickstart Agentforce 360 With Foundations

Strategically invest in AI that scales

64% of small businesses pay more for AI-powered applications because they see results. Smart leaders aren’t buying every new tool, they’re choosing the AI that‌ improves how they work overall.

How enterprises invest in AI: Big organizations can afford to experiment with dozens of AI tools. They can hire dedicated data science teams, and build custom solutions from scratch. They have separate budgets for pilot programs, proof-of-concepts, and full implementations. Their AI investments often take years to show results, but they can absorb those timelines and costs.

How SMBs invest in AI: Only 40% of small businesses currently use AI, but they start with solutions costing less than streaming services. They begin with simple tools like email workflows or chatbots, then gradually add capabilities as platforms evolve. Each successful step funds the next upgrade‌ — ‌sales teams automate lead qualification, marketing schedules, campaigns, service deployments, and response tools. The platform guides this progression, making advanced AI accessible without enterprise complexity.

  • Sales: AI sales tools help show your team which prospects are ready to buy and when to contact them and learn from your successful deals to predict future wins. 
  • Marketing: AI marketing tools create personalized experiences and A/B test campaigns before launch for better results. 
  • Service: AI service tools get instant answers to common questions and automatic routing for complex issues.

The smart move is to invest in a smart CRM now, and scale up to AI from there. A solid CRM foundation gives you clean data, and integrated workflows that make AI exponentially more powerful. By starting with a CRM for SMBs, you’ll prepare your data for advanced tools and technologies in the future, helping you fuel business growth faster. Starter Suite includes Data 360 features built-in, so you get both the CRM foundation and automation capabilities in one strategic investment that grows with your business.

Wherever you are — just get started.

No matter where you are on your journey as a business owner, you can get started with Starter Suite for free — the CRM made for growth.

Try it for free

Deliver enterprise customer experiences with AI

71% of SMBs report high or transformational value from AI customer service, making it the highest-impact AI application for SMBs. The customer experience becomes your competitive differentiator when deployed strategically.

How enterprises deliver customer experiences: Enterprises have AI-powered insights, dedicated customer experience teams, separate departments for sales enablement, marketing operations, and customer success. They can afford 24/7 call centers, personalized account managers, and sophisticated customer journey mapping. Their customer experience comes from having specialized people for every touchpoint and the right data to back it up.

How SMBs deliver customer experiences: SMBs are using ‌unified platforms to create seamless customer journeys that customers can’t distinguish from Fortune 500 companies. These teams consolidate sales, marketing, and service operations into integrated systems where every customer touchpoint builds on previous interactions. Automated lead scoring enables focused prospecting, while marketing automation delivers personalized engagement at scale. But it’s the service experience that really shines with the right CRM (shall we say 24/7 chatbot?).

Service automation resolves common issues instantly and routes complex cases to specialists. It creates the 24/7 support experience customers expect from big enterprises. AI chatbots answer questions immediately. Smart routing gets problems to the right person fast. Your customers get enterprise-level support from your small team. Modern customer service CRM platforms include these customer-facing smart tools, so you can deliver enterprise-quality experiences without hiring specialists or paying consulting fees.

Build a loyal customer base with smarter, faster service solutions.

Try it for free

Prepare for AI-powered jobs

Here’s the surprising truth: 59% of SMBs have created new jobs because of AI adoption, and 58% expect to create even more next year. AI isn’t replacing workers. It’s helping small businesses scale by creating space for higher-value work.

Thriving businesses aren’t downsizing — they’re growing more strategically. Yes, roles are shifting because, when AI handles routine tasks, team members can then focus on strategy — relationship building, strategic planning, and creative problem-solving.

AI elevates all roles — sales teams become strategic advisors when AI handles lead qualification. Marketers focus on campaign strategy while AI optimizes execution. Service agents solve complex problems while AI handles routine questions. Operations teams analyze trends instead of compiling reports.

How enterprises handle AI and workforce: Enterprises move through their workforce quickly, sometimes with high turnover. They can afford dedicated AI specialists, data scientists, and change management teams to handle workforce transitions. They create new roles as needed, with extensive planning and departmental coordination. Their scale allows them to shift people between divisions and absorb temporary inefficiencies during transitions.

How SMBs handle AI and workforce: Modern CRM platforms help small teams operate with enterprise-scale impact. With a human resources (HR) dedicated CRM, you can  surface insights that would take data science teams hours to find. Chatbots handle routine inquiries so your service team builds relationships. Marketing automates dozens of campaign variations simultaneously.

Job growth happens because AI-powered businesses can take on more customers, expand into new markets, and offer services that weren’t viable before. AI tools provide small teams with the capacity and speed that used to belong only to large enterprises.

How are SMBs outpacing enterprise? You guessed it: AI

Our research shows that businesses that moved on to AI early are already pulling ahead, operating like enterprises more and more every day. According to the latest edition of the Small and Medium Business Trends Report, 91% of SMBs with AI report revenue growth, and 90% say it makes their operations more efficient. They’re delivering enterprise-level experiences at small business speed. The competitive window is still open — but it’s closing fast.

You don’t need massive investments or data scientists. You need the right platform and the willingness to compete at an enterprise scale. The question isn’t whether AI will transform your industry. It’s whether you’ll lead that transformation or watch from the sidelines.Start your journey with Starter Suite today. Looking for more customization? Explore Pro Suite. Already a Salesforce customer? Activate Foundations and try out Agentforce 360 today.

AI supported the writers and editors who created this article.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

AI enterprise for small business means giving smaller teams access to advanced AI tools once limited to large corporations. With Salesforce Starter Suite and Pro Suite, small businesses get AI built into sales, service, and marketing so they can compete at a higher level. Learn more about Salesforce AI for small business.

AI enterprise solutions are now priced for smaller budgets. Salesforce Starter Suite begins at $25 per user per month with built-in AI. As businesses grow, they can move to Pro Suite or add tools like Agentforce 360 for customer service automation. See Salesforce pricing.

Yes. In fact, Salesforce’s Small & Medium Business Trends Report shows that over 70% of SMBs using AI report higher productivity and faster growth. With AI handling tasks like customer insights, content creation, and service response, small teams can scale more like large enterprises.

The most valuable features are AI-driven lead scoring, automated customer service, personalized campaigns, and predictive analytics. Salesforce AI includes these in Starter and Pro Suites so small businesses do not need extra tools.

No. Salesforce designs AI for small businesses with simple setup, guided onboarding, and integrations with popular apps. Most teams can start using AI features right away without technical expertise.
